opp.name: Avaya [S2P] June 2026
opp.next_step: Hi Reema, Colin, Attila, Michelle, Shrikant, and team, I hope you are all having a great week. I wanted to check back in following our initial RFI discussions and demonstrations. I know your team was planning to evaluate the full field of vendors to see how Zycus fits into your broader procurement strategy. Now that a bit of time has passed, I would love to reconnect briefly to get your feedback and see where we stand in your evaluation process. If you are ready for next steps, we can set up a quick 30-minute call to discuss: A tailored scoping session based on your feedback. Pricing sensitivities and ROI frameworks. Targeted deep dives into specific areas of interest (e.g., AP, supplier onboarding, or CLM). Please let me know if you have any 30-minute windows open later this week or next for a quick sync, or if there is an updated timeline you can share from the Avaya side. Looking forward to hearing from you!
opp.ais_why:

• The Agentic-platform-discussed flag is not set and there is no AI-requirement rating, so AI has not surfaced as a buyer priority.
• Next step is simply to 'reconnect for feedback and discuss next steps,' with no evidence of AI-driven motivation in the conversation.

metrics.narrative: Buyer articulated qualitative pain but no quantified business case. Shrikant (2026-06-02) described the problem: current system is 'bloated, heavily customized,' causing manual 'swivel chair integration' that leads to 'time consumption, human error, more point of failures.' Attila emphasized supplier onboarding as 'the biggest challenge' and 'we really would like to improve on that one' (2026-06-02). Buyer seeks 'streamlined, modern S2P platform' to replace fragmented stack. Cost motivation confirmed: buyer replacing Coupa because it is 'too expensive relative to the value they provide' (Shrikant, 2026-06-11 demo). However, NO quantified metrics: no cycle-time reduction target, error-rate baseline, FTE savings, or ROI number. Budget allocated internally but envelope undisclosed. Without a quantified value case, Zycus cannot anchor pricing to measurable savings, leaving the door open for a lower-cost competitor or Coupa price concession to win on cost alone.

champion.narrative: No champion identified; best candidate is developing but has not yet acted as advocate. Attila Abraham (Purchasing Manager EMEA, GSO operations) is the strongest candidate: he participated in both discovery (2026-06-02) and demo (2026-06-11), emphasized supplier onboarding as his top pain, disclosed that 'Zycus came into picture kind of last minute' and urged Zycus to 'catch up' by accelerating demo timing ('any help on that front to see it Monday, Tuesday would appreciate it,' 2026-06-02). He has operational stake (GSO operations, supplier onboarding owner) and engaged actively on calls. However, he has NOT yet acted as advocate: no evidence of coaching Zycus on competitive threats, introducing Zycus to CIO/Akumark, or selling Zycus internally against other shortlisted vendors. Reema Gupta (procurement lead, RFI process owner) is engaged but neutral: she is running a formal multi-vendor evaluation and has not shown preference. Shrikant (IT lead) is engaged on technical fit but has not advocated. CIO and Akumark (decision-makers) are not engaged at all. Without a champion to sell Zycus internally, coach on decision criteria, and drive the shortlist recommendation, Zycus is at risk of being out-sold by a competitor who recruits an internal advocate first. Developing a champion—most likely Attila—is the highest-leverage near-term move.

competition.narrative: Competitive landscape partially known; Coupa confirmed as incumbent being displaced, other shortlisted vendors unknown. Coupa (SAP subsidiary) is the named incumbent: buyer uses 'Coupa for indirect procurement' (Shrikant, 2026-06-02) and is replacing it because it is 'too expensive relative to the value they provide' (Shrikant, 2026-06-11). Buyer also mentioned 'current tools primarily Coupa/SAP' (Next Step 2026-06-19), implying SAP Ariba may also be in the stack or evaluated historically, though Ramesh clarified (2026-06-02) 'Ariba was there, but we still use Ariba more as a customer, not as a vendor'—so Ariba is not the active incumbent. Buyer disclosed (Reema, 2026-06-02) 'we plan to issue RFI to Zycus and other vendors we have shortlisted,' confirming multiple vendors in the running, but did NOT name the other shortlisted vendors. Competitive field beyond Coupa is a black box. Zycus wedge: full S2P suite on single code base (vs. Coupa's fragmented modules), AI embedded in workflow (Merlin Intake, AI sourcing, Merlin Risk Radar, CLM Insta Review) as buyer explicitly requested 'AI embedded in workflow not bolt-on,' and supplier onboarding automation (Attila's top pain). Activation: quantify ROI vs. Coupa's cost, demonstrate single-platform TCO advantage, recruit Attila as champion to sell these differentiators internally, and engage CIO/Akumark to validate strategic fit before shortlist decision (imminent, early July 2026).

decision_process.narrative: Buyer disclosed formal multi-vendor RFI/shortlist/selection process. Reema (2026-06-02): 'We plan to issue an RFI with NDA to Zycus and other shortlisted vendors, then shortlist vendors we want to work with.' Shrikant: 'Do evaluation in a week or two, shortlist from RFI, move forward with reference checks, deeper dive demos.' Timeline: 'kick off this fiscal year itself.' Process sequence confirmed: (1) RFI issued to multiple shortlisted vendors (completed by Zycus ~2026-06-03, demo 2026-06-11); (2) vendor demos (Zycus completed 2026-06-11); (3) shortlist decision (imminent, expected by early July 2026 based on 'week or two' from 2026-06-02); (4) deeper evaluation (references, commercials, ROI) for shortlisted vendors; (5) CIO and Akumark/Reema leadership final sign-off; (6) contracting and project kickoff 'this fiscal year.' Approval chain: IT (Shrikant, CIO sign-off) and GSO/procurement (Reema, Akumark leadership sign-off). Zycus entered late ('came into picture kind of last minute,' Attila 2026-06-02) and is catching up to other vendors who started earlier. Shortlist gate is the immediate next milestone; Zycus must secure a shortlist slot to remain in contention.

Avoma call 2026-06-11 demo: Merlin Intake, AI sourcing, Merlin Risk Radar, CLM Insta Review, P2P/AP validated against buyer criteria
decision_criteria.narrative: Buyer articulated explicit evaluation criteria across calls and Next Step. From 2026-06-02 call: (1) 'streamlined, modern S2P platform' replacing bloated/customized incumbent; (2) full source-to-pay integration (sourcing, procurement, supplier onboarding, CLM, selective AP) on single code base to eliminate manual/swivel-chair integration; (3) supplier onboarding automation (end-to-end workflow, supplier-side data entry, automatic SAP vendor record creation) as top priority; (4) 'value for money versus current tools (primarily Coupa/SAP) without sacrificing functionality' (Next Step 2026-06-19); (5) AI embedded in workflow, not bolt-on (Next Step 2026-06-19); (6) flexibility on integrations, custom fields, invoice handling (non-PO and SAP PO flows) (Next Step 2026-06-19); (7) SAP ERP integration (SAP is system of record). Demo 2026-06-11 validated fit on Merlin Intake (guided buying, maverick spend reduction), AI-enabled sourcing (RFI/RFP generation, autonomous tail-spend), supplier management (Merlin Risk Radar), CLM (Insta Review, redlining, Word authoring), and P2P/AP. Buyer confirmed 'looking to improve value for money' and 'AI embedded in workflow' as decision filters. Criteria are buyer-stated and Zycus-confirmed; no criteria gaps.

ai_fit_signal.summary: Buyer is AI Curious. Explicit requirement for 'AI embedded in workflow rather than as a bolt-on' (Next Step 2026-06-19) and positive reception of Merlin capabilities (Intake, AI sourcing, Risk Radar, CLM Insta Review) during 2026-06-11 demo confirm buyer sees AI as a decision criterion and differentiator vs. incumbent Coupa. However, buyer did not articulate a quantified AI ROI target or ask for AI-specific use-case validation (e.g. measured maverick-spend reduction via Merlin Intake, supplier onboarding cycle-time improvement via AI workflow automation). AI is a stated filter ('embedded not bolt-on') but not yet the primary driver; cost-vs-value and supplier onboarding automation are the primary motivators. Tier: AI Curious (interested, values AI as embedded capability, but not yet quantifying AI-specific ROI or making AI the decision anchor).

flags: Champion development: Attila Abraham is the most engaged and vocal buyer contact (supplier onboarding pain owner, operationally invested, drove demo urgency) but has not yet demonstrated active advocacy or internal selling. Build a one-on-one relationship with Attila, provide him with a tailored supplier onboarding ROI model or automation proof point (e.g. 'eliminate 80% of manual onboarding tasks, reduce onboarding cycle time from X days to Y days'), and position him as the operational sponsor who validates fit and drives vendor selection internally. If his upward influence is limited, ask him to introduce Zycus to Akumark or the CIO for an executive-level technical or value briefing.

Economic Buyer and Decision Maker are unmapped and unengaged: CIO (John Hill per contact list, not verified on calls) and Akumark (GSO/procurement final authority, full name/title not disclosed) have not appeared on any call or activity, yet they are the final sign-offs per Shrikant (2026-06-02). Zycus is selling to mid-level evaluators (Reema, Shrikant, Attila) who will recommend but do not control budget or final decision. Risk: if CIO or Akumark favor another vendor or the incumbent, mid-level evaluators cannot override. Move: request executive connect (deal owner's manager) to CIO and Akumark, framed as 'validating strategic fit and implementation partnership at the executive level' (not a sales push). Use the follow-up call (week of 2026-06-30) to ask Reema and Shrikant to facilitate the introduction, or go direct via LinkedIn/email if they will not.

Competitive positioning and TCO model: Coupa is the incumbent and primary threat; buyer is cost-sensitive and flagged Coupa as too expensive. Deliver a Coupa-displacement battle card and TCO comparison model showing: (a) Coupa annual cost vs Zycus annual cost (SaaS + services), (b) system consolidation savings (eliminate adjacent tools Coupa requires), (c) supplier onboarding efficiency ROI (quantified time/cost savings from automation), (d) Merlin AI value (capabilities Coupa lacks or charges premium for). Provide a reference customer (ideally Panasonic North America, mentioned on 2 Jun call as recent similar win, or another Coupa-displacement customer) who switched for similar reasons. Position this as a decision-support tool for the RFI shortlist evaluation.
best_practice_check.summary: Three highest-leverage win-strategy moves: (1) Recruit Attila Abraham as champion before shortlist decision (imminent, early July 2026) — he owns the top pain (supplier onboarding), disclosed Zycus late entry, and urged acceleration; without a champion Zycus risks being out-sold by a competitor who recruits an internal advocate first. (2) Quantify ROI/TCO vs. Coupa to anchor 'value for money' claim — buyer is cost-sensitive and replacing Coupa on price-vs-value grounds; without a quantified savings model a lower-cost competitor or Coupa price concession can win. (3) Engage CIO (John Hill) and Akumark (GSO leadership) directly to validate strategic fit and decision criteria at the level where shortlist and vendor-selection decisions are made — Zycus is currently selling to mid-level evaluators who recommend but do not control the final decision.

competitive_position.summary: Coupa is the named incumbent being displaced on cost-vs-value grounds; the strongest current threat. Buyer replacing Coupa because 'too expensive relative to value they provide' (Shrikant 2026-06-11), but Coupa remains a threat if it drops price to retain the account. Other shortlisted vendors unknown (buyer disclosed 'other vendors we have shortlisted' but did not name them, Reema 2026-06-02); competitive landscape beyond Coupa is a black box, preventing tailored differentiation. Zycus entered late ('last minute,' Attila 2026-06-02) and must catch up. Without knowing the other shortlisted vendors or Coupa's retention strategy, Zycus cannot position against the full competitive field. Immediate risk: a lower-cost challenger or Coupa price concession wins if Zycus does not anchor pricing to quantified savings and recruit a champion before shortlist decision (imminent, early July 2026).

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Strong fit on stated decision criteria; Zycus capabilities align well with buyer's explicit requirements. Buyer seeks (1) streamlined modern S2P replacing bloated/customized incumbent, (2) full integration on single code base to eliminate manual/swivel-chair work, (3) supplier onboarding automation (top priority), (4) value for money vs. Coupa/SAP without sacrificing functionality, (5) AI embedded in workflow not bolt-on, (6) flexibility on integrations/custom fields/invoice handling, (7) SAP ERP integration. Zycus validated fit on all criteria during 2026-06-11 demo: Merlin Intake (guided buying, maverick spend reduction), AI sourcing (RFI/RFP generation, autonomous tail-spend), supplier onboarding (end-to-end workflow, auto SAP vendor creation), CLM (Insta Review redlining, Word authoring), P2P/AP (non-PO and SAP PO flows). Review RFI and Commercials with Avaya - follow up next week for next steps


Hi Reema, Colin, Attila, Michelle, Shrikant, and team,


Thank you again for your time and the thoughtful questions during today’s session.

By way of quick recap, we walked through the full Zycus source-to-pay suite, focusing on:

  • Very important — How Merlin Intake and guided buying simplify end‑user purchasing, help reduce maverick spend, and improve user adoption versus traditional eProcurement.
  • Sourcing capabilities across RFIs/RFPs/RFQs and auctions, including embedded AI to generate questionnaires, benchmark and score supplier responses, and autonomous sourcing for “three bids and a buy” tail‑spend scenarios.
  • Supplier management and risk (including Merlin Risk Radar), and how onboarding, risk events, and scorecards can support your governance and due‑diligence needs.
  • End‑to‑end CLM with clause and template libraries, Insta Review for third‑party paper, WordConnect for authoring in Microsoft Word, and contract utilization tracking tied directly to PR/PO activity.
  • Procure‑to‑pay and AP (PR→PO→GR/Service Receipt→Invoice), AP SmartDesk for invoice ingestion and exception handling, and analytics/benchmarking to track process performance against best‑in‑class KPIs.

You also shared some important context and priorities:

  • You’re looking to improve value for money versus your current tools (primarily Coupa/SAP) without sacrificing functionality, and you want AI to be embedded in the workflow rather than as a bolt‑on.
  • Indirect spend, PR‑driven purchasing, and a cleaner supplier onboarding process (with SAP as system of record) are key focus areas, along with flexibility on integrations, custom fields, and invoice handling (including non‑PO and SAP PO flows).


Next Steps:

  • Owner: Edward – I will propose a few time options for a follow‑up call to discuss potential next steps, including scoping session, pricing sensitivities, ROI, and any areas where you’d like a deeper dive (e.g., AP, supplier onboarding, or CLM).
    • Pick any 1/2 hr that works for you:
      • June 18th - 11:00 AM to 1:00 PM ET
  • June 19th - 10:00 AM to 12:00 PM ET

  • Owner: Avaya (Reema / Colin / Attila) – You’ll continue with your other vendor demos as part of the RFI process and let us know how Zycus fits in once you’ve seen the full field.
  • Owner: Edward – I’ll also share today’s slide deck (see attached), and if helpful, we can coordinate references
  • and provide our recording if you need a backup to your own.
